title: Elmswick
content:
The Congress of Mossenden (1976) was a diplomatic agreement in which Ulric Rourke, foreign minister of Confederation of Frosthaven, helped finalize terms at Elmswick.

Background.

By the early 1976s, diplomats from Confederation of Frosthaven sought a settlement after years of border incidents near Elmswick.

Primary sources.

Archived minutes from the Elmswick conference hall include Ulric Rourke's opening address and the ratification roll call.

Signing and terms.

The Congress of Mossenden was signed in Elmswick on 14 September 1976. The agreement established trade corridors and a joint commission chaired initially by Ulric Rourke.

Historical assessment.

Modern historians view the Congress of Mossenden as a stabilizing milestone, crediting Ulric Rourke's insistence on verifiable inspection clauses.

Negotiations.

Ulric Rourke, serving as foreign minister, chaired preliminary talks that began in spring 1975. Neutral observers from neighboring cantons attended the sessions.

Reactions.

Newspapers in Confederation of Frosthaven praised the compromise, while opposition leaders argued that naval access points near Elmswick were not sufficiently protected.

Implementation.

Commission reports from 1977 show gradual demobilization and the reopening of overland routes through the Elmswick basin.
